meaning: adj.不合格的,无资格的,没限制的,绝对的
examples: She was unqualified for the job.
Norway's adoption of a nationwide corporate gender quota has led to the entry of unqualified candidates into the board.
I gave her my unqualified support.
Whatever the mixed effects elsewhere, it has been an unqualified win for American wine buyers.
But there is still the reality that not every Saarinen building was an unqualified success.
